title: USD Value Resolution Spec Coverage Ledger

normative_source.rule: Only the AOUSD core specification at aousd/specifications-public v1.0.1 is normative for this ledger. OpenUSD behavior is not a source for these requirements.

status_values.implemented: In scope for this value-resolution contract and covered by goldens.
status_values.out_of_scope: Belongs to composition, parsing, asset resolution, or stage population rather than value resolution.

coverage_rules: The resolver consumes composed strong-to-weak opinion stacks; it does not build composition indexes.
The resolver consumes typed document-model field values and does not parse USDA syntax or canonical JSON dumps as its durable value model.
Value resolution claims require independent contract goldens for metadata, attribute values, listOps, splines, clips, retiming, schema fallbacks, and relationship target behavior.

obligations: Consume a strength-ordered composed opinion stack.
Preserve typed source provenance in resolver outputs.
Keep composition, stage population, and value resolution as separate graph-owned steps.
Resolve default-time attribute values by walking authored default opinions strongest to weakest.
Stop at authored value blocks and blocked time samples without considering weaker values.
Acquire schema fallback values from typed schemas, applied schemas, and default metadata.
Resolve exact authored time samples.
Implement held and linear interpolation according to the stage interpolation mode.
Apply layer offset and scale before source-sample lookup.
Resolve ordinary metadata from the strongest authored opinion.
Apply AOUSD special rules for specifier, typeName, variability, and custom.
Combine dictionaries recursively.
Combine generic metadata listOps, including targetPaths and connectionPaths.
Resolve layer metadata from the root layer spec only.
Evaluate spline knots before defaults and clips for timed queries.
Honor held, linear, curve, and value-block spline segment interpolation.
Honor spline extrapolation where a query falls outside authored knot times.
Find active clips and map stage time to clip time through the times metadata.
Open and query clip layer data supplied to the resolver context.
Use manifest defaults and interpolate missing clip values when configured.
Apply clip strength between local data and weaker referenced data.
Resolve raw relationship targetPaths through generic listOp metadata.
Resolve forwarded relationship targets recursively.
Resolve attribute connectionPaths as raw connection targets without forwarding.
